folio.label: 124v
folio.l: And coude answere hi~ , in his ledne ageyn
Hatú vnderstonden , what this faucon seide
And wel neigú for the routhe , almest she deiede
And to the tree , she gotú ful hastily
And on this faucon , loketú pytously
And held hire lappe a brod , for wel she wiste
The faucon must fallen , fro the twiste
Whan that it swougúnetú next , for lak of blood
A longe while , to wayten hire she stood
Til at the laste , she spak in this manere
Vn to the hauke , as ©e shuln after heere
What is the cause , if that it be to telle
That ©e ben , in this furyal peyne of helle
Quod Canacee , vn to this hauke a boue
Is this for sorwe of detú , or losse of loue
For as I trowe , theise ben causes two
That causen moost , a gentil herte woo
Of other harm , it nedetú nat to speke
For ©e ©oure self , vpon ©oure self ©ow wreke
Whicú preeuetú wel , that either Ire or drede
Mote ben enchesou~ , of ©oure cruel dede
Syn that I se , noon oæä wygút , ©ow chace
For loue of god , as dotú ©oure seluen grace
Or what may ben ©oure helpe , for west nortú est
Ne saw I neuäe or now , no bryd nor beest
That ferde witú hi~ self , so pitously
ñe slee me witú ©oure sorwe , verreyly
I haue of ©ow , so gret compassiou~
For goddes loue , come fro the tree adoun~
And as I am , a kynges dougúter trewe
If that I verreyly , the causes knewe
Of ©oure dissese , if it lay in my mygút
I wolde amende it , or that it were nygút
As wisly helpe me , gret god of kynde
And herbes shal I , rigút I nowe fynde
To hele witú ©oure hurtes , hastily
Tho shrigút this faucouö , ©et more pitously
Than euäe she dide , & fel to grounde a non
And litú a swougúne , as ded as litú a ston~
Til Canacee , hatú in hire lappe , hire take
In to that tyme , she gan of swougú a wake
And after that , she of swou©nyngö gan a breyde
Rigút in hire haukes ledne , thus she seide
¢ That pitee rennetú sone , in gentil herte
Felyngö his similitude , in peynes smerte
Is preeued alday , as men moun it se
